sourceMovie.title: Men
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Men (2022) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Alex Garland's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

seoTitle: 10 Stunning Movies Like Men (Folk Horror & Dread Picks)
seoDescription: If you loved Men’s surreal folk horror, these 10 films match its unease, metaphor-heavy dread, and unforgettable atmosphere—ranked with streaming notes.
seoH1: Because Men Left You Uneasy: 10 Films to Chase That Feeling
customIntroParagraphs: Men weaponizes repetition and the English countryside: Garland turns every encounter into a mirror, stretching dread through body horror and allegory until the film feels like a bad dream you cannot fact-check. It is divisive on purpose—less about plot receipts, more about mood sticking under your skin.

These ten picks chase that same surreal folk-horror frequency: uncanny villages, identity horror, and tension that lingers after the credits. Use the related guides to hop toward The Unhealer, Hereditary, Get Out, or The Conjuring when you want more curated horror trails without leaving the site.

shortComparison.heading: Men vs Hereditary — key differences
shortComparison.body: Men stretches folk-horror unease and repetition until metaphor takes over. Hereditary keeps grief and lineage in surgical focus—every room is a trap you can diagram. Both punish denial; Garland dissolves the floor while Aster tightens the walls.

midPageYouMightAlsoLike.anchorText: Folk dread in the open air (Midsommar)
Isolation tech-thriller with body-horror edges (Ex Machina)
Paranoia in a hostile landscape (The Witch)
Unease in rooms that should feel safe (Parasite)
Folk-adjacent dread that refuses tidy explanations (The Unhealer)

recommendations.title: Spell
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Men clicked for you, Spell (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Men?
faq.answer: Try Spell, The Witch, Pan's Labyrinth—each shares DNA with Men while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Men?
faq.answer: Men (2022) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Men worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
